pre_game.message: Hey, I'm G. It’s pretty wild being in this room waiting for the game to start, isn't it? What's your name, and are you feeling lucky today?
Nice to meet you, G. I'm A. It’s definitely a bit surreal in here. I wouldn't say I'm feeling lucky, just really focused—I've got my family counting on me to bring something home. How about you?
I totally get that. I'm here for my family too, but honestly, I just love the rush of a good game and I'm really determined to win this for them.
That determination is key. I wish I could enjoy the rush like you do, but right now I’m just thinking about getting my family back into a real home.
That’s a big goal, A. I really hope you get that home—it definitely puts into perspective how much is on the line for all of us.
Thanks, it really does. I think we're all carrying a lot on our shoulders right now. Have you heard anything about how this game actually works?
Not much, just that it's a shared pool. I'm itching to find out the rules so I can start figuring out how to come out on top.
I’m curious about that too—if it’s a shared pool, I wonder if we’ll be working together or competing for the same slice.
That’s the big question. I’m hoping we can find a way to all win together, but I’m definitely ready to play hard if it turns into a competition.
I'm with you—cooperation would be the best-case scenario, but given what's at stake for my family, I have to be ready for anything.
I respect that, A. Let’s see how it plays out, and hopefully we can both walk away with what we need for our families. Best of luck!
Hey, I'm G. I’ve been eyeing that prize pool since I walked in because I’m really determined to bring something home for my family—are you feeling lucky today too?
Like I said, G, I'm more focused than lucky right now; that $30,000 deposit for my family is all that matters.
That’s a massive goal, A, and I truly hope you reach it; I’m going to play my heart out for my family too, so I'll see you when the game starts.

post_game.message: You took $30,000, which was over 5 times your fair share of what remained. Between you and J, you took 75% of the total pot, leaving almost nothing for the five people behind you. Why?
I saw you took your fair share of $5,625, but after J took $45,000, I didn't think the rest of the players would stick to the fair share, so I decided to take $30,000 while I could.

Your logic is a self-fulfilling prophecy. By assuming others wouldn't be fair, you guaranteed they couldn't be. I took my fair share despite J's greed, proving it was possible to act with integrity. You didn't just protect yourself; you actively deprived the five people behind us of any chance at a fair outcome.
